sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Grouse Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1). This is the Grouse Lake just inside the Desolation Wilderness boundary above Wrights Lake (El Dorado Co.), the lowest of the Grouse–Hemlock–Smith lake chain reached from the Wrights Lake / Rockbound Trailhead — not the Grouse Lakes near Grouse Ridge in Nevada Co. It drains west via Wrights Lake to Silver Creek and the South Fork American River on the Pacific slope — not into the Lake Tahoe basin — so the Lake Tahoe tributaries special regulation §7.50(b)(152), which closes Tahoe tributaries seasonally 'upstream to the first lake,' does not reach it; the lake follows the general lake regulation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.description: Grouse Lake lies inside Desolation Wilderness (Wrights Lake / Rockbound Trailhead access). A USDA Forest Service wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use; an overnight quota applies from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30. Day use is free and not limited by quota. Bear canisters are required for all overnight visitors.

raw.overnight_quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30

regulations.citation: USDA Forest Service — Desolation Wilderness permit program
regulations.humanSummary: Desolation Wilderness permit: required year-round for overnight use (quota Fri. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30); day use free and un-quota'd.
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

parameters.description: Fishery note. A Sierra Nevada lake-fishing database (Sierrafish.com) describes Grouse Lake as 'a self-sustaining fishery of Brook Trout that live in Desolation Wilderness.' CDFW does not list Grouse Lake in its 2026 Provisional Stocking plan and has ended aerial stocking of Desolation Wilderness lakes, so any brook trout are a self-sustaining remnant of historical plantings, not a current put-and-take fishery. This is a third-party angler database, not a CDFW fish survey.

regulations.citation: Sierrafish.com — Grouse Lake (Desolation Wilderness); CDFW 2026 Provisional Stocking Document (Grouse Lake not listed)
regulations.humanSummary: Reported as a self-sustaining brook-trout water by a third-party angler database; not currently stocked by CDFW.
regulations.verbatimText: Grouse Lake is a self-sustaining fishery of Brook Trout that live in Desolation Wilderness.
